title: Dewy Crisp Uruguayan Tomato Tart
description: A bright and refreshing tart that showcases ripe confit tomatoes atop a crisp crust, finished with a herbaceous garlic powder glaze; it is kid-friendly and crafted in a terracotta dish for a rustic finish.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Add cubed butter and cut into the flour with a pastry cutter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Pour in ice water, stir gently, and form a dough without overmixing.
Roll the dough out to a 12-inch circle and lay it in a terracotta baking dish.
Brush the dough with olive oil and sprinkle garlic powder, oregano, and basil evenly over the surface.
Arrange sliced confitted tomatoes over the dough, leaving a small border.
Top with sliced cherry tomatoes and finish with a light dusting of lemon zest.
Bake at 375°F for 25 minutes until the crust is golden and the tomatoes are bubbly.
Let the tart cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm as a lunch or snack.
